q: Last summer we ____ to Spain for our holiday. (go)
The plane ____ at the airport two hours late yesterday. (arrive)
We ____ all our clothes into one big suitcase last night. (put)
On the first day of the trip I ____ my passport at the hotel. (lose)
They ____ lots of photos when they visited the old castle. (take)
Look, the children are ____ in the sea right now. (swim)
Be quiet, please. Dad is ____ the map to find our hotel. (read)
I can't talk now because we ____ packing our bags for the flight. (be)
My sister has ____ to London three times this year. (be)
I have never ____ such a beautiful beach before. (see)
We can't check in because Mum ____ lost the tickets.
The beach in Italy was ____ than the one near our house. (clean)
This is the ____ hotel we have ever stayed in. (expensive)
Travelling by plane is much ____ than travelling by bus. (fast)
Next week we are ____ to visit my grandparents in the countryside. (go)
I think it ____ rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella on the trip.
We didn't have ____ money left after we bought the souvenirs.
There were too ____ tourists at the museum, so we couldn't see the paintings.
Have you got ____ water? I'm really thirsty after the long walk.
Our flight leaves ____ Monday morning, so we must wake up early.
The tour bus arrives ____ nine o'clock, so don't be late.
This suitcase is yours, and the small red one is ____.
We've found our tickets, but the boys still can't find ____.
You ____ wear your seatbelt on the plane; it is a strict rule.
You ____ drink the tap water here because it can make you ill.
I wanted to swim, ____ the sea was far too cold.
We missed the train, ____ we had to wait an hour for the next one.
You keep your money and passport safe in this small bag when you travel; it is called a ____.
The person who checks your passport and bags before a flight at the airport works in passport ____.
A book or picture that shows roads, towns and countries to help you find your way is called a ____.
What ____ you do yesterday after school? (do)
Last weekend we ____ a new video game on my brother's console. (play)
She ____ her old phone to her cousin last month. (give)
I ____ a funny video online and laughed a lot last night. (watch)
The teacher ____ us how to code a simple robot in class yesterday. (teach)
Be quiet, please. Dad ____ having a video call with his boss right now. (be)
Look at the screen! The battery icon ____ flashing red. (be)
My little sister and I ____ watching a cartoon on the tablet at the moment. (be)
I have already ____ my new headphones twice today. (use)
She has ____ her tablet on the bus, so now she is very upset. (lose)
We have never ____ this app before, so we don't know how it works. (use)
My new laptop is much ____ than my old one. (fast)
A smartphone is ____ expensive than a basic phone. (expensive)
This is the ____ camera in the whole shop. (good)
I think robots ____ help us with homework in the future.
Look at those dark clouds. I am ____ to charge my phone before the storm. (go)
There isn't ____ space left on my phone for more photos.
How ____ apps have you downloaded on your new tablet?
I have only a ____ battery left, so I'll turn the screen off.
My family always video-calls Grandma ____ Sunday evenings.
The new game console comes out ____ December.
That charger isn't mine; I think it's ____.
These wireless earphones are ____, not your brother's.
You ____ charge your phone before the trip, or it will die.
If you want to save your work, you ____ click the save button.
I wanted to play online, ____ the internet stopped working.
She bought a new phone case ____ her old one was broken.
You can type messages on this small phone you hold in your hand, called a ____.
The flat device with a touch screen, smaller than a laptop, is a ____.
You wear these on or in your ears to listen to music; they are called ____.
Last weekend we ____ the local river and saw lots of fish swimming. (visit)
The farmer ____ many trees in the empty field last spring. (plant)
The children ____ a baby bird that had fallen out of its nest. (find)
It ____ heavily all night, so the ground was very wet this morning. (rain)
The strong wind ____ the leaves off the old oak tree yesterday. (blow)
Look, the ice on the lake ____ because the weather is getting warmer. (melt)
Be quiet, some deer ____ near the edge of the forest right now. (stand)
My sister ____ the flowers in the garden at the moment. (water)
The plant has ____ a lot since we put it near the sunny window. (grow)
Scientists ____ found a new kind of insect deep in the rainforest.
Our town ____ already cleaned up all the rubbish from the beach.
A mountain is much ____ than a small green hill. (high)
Recycling paper is one of the ____ ways to protect the planet. (easy)
Air pollution in big cities is ____ dangerous than in the countryside. (dangerous)
Next year our class is ____ to plant a hundred trees in the park. (go)
If we save water now, the future ____ be much better for everyone.
There isn't ____ water left in the dry river during the summer.
How ____ animals live in this big national park?
We don't have ____ time before the nature walk begins, so let's hurry.
In winter the lake often freezes ____ December.
We are going hiking in the mountains ____ Saturday morning.
This recycling bin is ours, and that green one over there is ____.
I forgot my water bottle, so Tom kindly let me drink some of ____.
You ____ never drop plastic bottles into the sea because it harms the fish.
To save energy at home, we ____ turn off the lights when we leave a room.
The garden was very dry, ____ we gave the plants a lot of water.
I really wanted to see the waterfall, ____ the path was closed for repairs.
The tall, cold place at the top of a mountain that is often covered with snow is called a ____.
A large area of land covered with many trees, where wild animals live, is a ____.
When we put paper, glass and plastic in special bins to use them again, we call this ____.
Yesterday I ____ off my bike and hurt my knee. (fall)
The doctor ____ me some medicine for my cough last week. (give)
My sister ____ her arm when she was playing football. (break)
We ____ our teeth before we went to bed last night. (brush)
He ____ very tired after the long walk in the mountains. (feel)
Be quiet please, the nurse ____ the baby's temperature right now. (check)
Look at Tom, he ____ his hands at the sink. (wash)
My parents ____ in the park to keep healthy this morning. (run)
I have ____ my finger and now it is bleeding. (cut)
She ____ already eaten her breakfast, so she is not hungry now.
They have ____ a lot of water after the race. (drink)
Carrots are ____ for your eyes than sweets. (healthy)
My toothache today is ____ than it was yesterday. (bad)
This is the ____ hospital in our whole city. (good)
If you eat too much sugar, you ____ get a stomachache later.
I feel sick, so I am ____ to stay in bed all day tomorrow. (go)
The doctor said I do not need to take ____ medicine because I am better now.
You should drink ____ water every day to stay healthy.
How ____ hours of sleep did you get last night?
I have a doctor's appointment ____ Monday morning.
The chemist's shop opens ____ eight o'clock in the morning.
My toothbrush is blue and the red one is ____.
That medicine belongs to my brother, so it is ____.
You have a high fever, so you ____ see a doctor immediately.
In the hospital you ____ be quiet because other people are sleeping.
I went to bed early ____ I was feeling very ill.
She broke her leg, ____ she could not play in the match.
You use this part of your body to smell flowers and it is in the middle of your face. It is your ____.
When you are ill and have a very hot body, the doctor says you have a ____.
This part of your body pumps blood and you can feel it beating in your chest. It is your ____.
Yesterday we ____ at a lovely new restaurant in town. (eat)
My mum ____ a delicious chocolate cake for my birthday last week. (make)
We ____ our breakfast quickly because we were late for school. (finish)
The waiter ____ us a big menu when we sat down. (bring)
I ____ some fresh orange juice this morning before I left home. (drink)
Look, the chef is ____ a huge pizza in the kitchen right now. (cook)
Be quiet, Grandma is ____ her soup at the table. (eat)
We ____ having lunch in the garden because it is sunny today. (be)
I have already ____ all my vegetables, so can I have some pudding? (eat)
My sister ____ never tried sushi before today.
The waiter has ____ us the bill, so we must pay now. (give)
Honey is much ____ than lemon, so I prefer it in my tea. (sweet)
This is the ____ curry I have ever eaten in my whole life. (spicy)
Pizza is the most ____ food on the whole menu; everyone loves it. (delicious)
Tomorrow we are ____ to eat at my favourite burger place. (go)
Don't worry, I ____ help you carry the heavy shopping bags.
There isn't ____ milk left, so we cannot make a milkshake.
How ____ apples do we need to bake the apple pie?
I am a bit hungry, so I will have just a ____ of those crisps.
We always have a big family dinner ____ Sunday afternoon.
The restaurant opens ____ six o'clock in the evening.
This sandwich is not yours, it is ____, so please give it back to me.
Tom ate his cake quickly, but Lucy is still eating ____.
You ____ wash your hands before you eat your dinner.
If you want to be healthy, you ____ eat more fruit and vegetables.
I didn't order a dessert ____ I was already too full.
The soup was very hot, ____ I waited a few minutes before eating it.
The sweet course you eat at the end of a meal, like ice cream or cake, is called ____.
The person who brings your food to the table in a restaurant is called a ____.
The piece of paper that tells you how much money you must pay in a restaurant is called the ____.
